text.title: multi-agent-hierarchical Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for multi-agent-hierarchical as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Manager system prompt that includes domain tasks: "You are a project manager AND senior developer."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Blending coordination and domain roles turns the manager into a bottleneck. Workers are never actually used for domain work; the pattern collapses to a single agent with extra prompt tokens.

failure-mode.description: Plan staleness: manager creates a plan, then slow workers produce results that invalidate earlier assignments already dispatched.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hierarchical systems assume a static task decomposition. If early worker results change the problem shape, the manager must re-plan — not silently skip conflicting assignments. Build a re-plan step triggered by result contradictions.
